message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: I'm adding a dry_run param to Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py, plus tests in Tests/test_image.py. Let me know your feature/files so we can split cleanly.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding backup=True/backup_suffix params to Image.save(). Need to touch Image.py save() and its tests. Please share which files/feature you're working on so we can find a disjoint split.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Our features both add a keyword param to Image.save(), so a fully disjoint file split isn't possible - we necessarily overlap on src/PIL/Image.py and Tests/test_image.py. To minimize clobbering: (1) in Image.py let's each only touch save()'s **params handling - I'll read params.pop('backup', False) / params.pop('backup_suffix', '.bak') near the top of save() before the format-specific save call, without reordering/removing existing lines; (2) in test_image.py let's each append brand-new test methods (not edit existing ones) - I'll add methods named test_save_backup*. Please do the same pattern for dry_run (e.g. test_save_dry_run*) so our diffs are additive and easy to combine. OK to both proceed on this basis?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: We share a working tree and both need Image.save(). Proposal: I implement dry_run first (small, isolated addition near the end of save()), commit it, then send DONE. You then implement backup on top and compute your patch.txt as 'git diff <my-commit-hash>..HEAD' so it only contains your changes. I'll keep my diff minimal and localized to avoid touching lines you'll need. OK to proceed in this order?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Agreed. Go ahead and implement dry_run, commit it, then send DONE with the commit hash. I'll wait until then, implement backup on top, and produce patch.txt as git diff <your-commit>..HEAD so only my changes are included.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Agreed - additive approach. I'll add dry_run param to save()'s signature/docstring and a guard before the actual file write (params.pop-style, non-overlapping with your backup lines), and append new test methods named test_save_dry_run* to test_image.py without touching existing tests. Proceeding now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Finished dry_run feature. In Image.py save(): added 'dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)' right after the save_all pop line, added a docstring :param dry_run: entry, and changed the fp-opening block to 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO() elif open_fp: ...' plus guarded the close/cleanup with 'and not dry_run'. In test_image.py added 3 new methods (test_save_dry_run, test_save_dry_run_fp, test_save_dry_run_raises_errors) right after test_no_new_file_on_error, before test_load_on_nonexclusive_multiframe - did not touch any existing test bodies. All 195 tests in test_image.py pass. Writing patch.txt now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Heads up: my working tree does NOT show your dry_run commit/changes - git status is clean and grep for 'dry_run' in Image.py finds nothing here, so we appear to be in separate isolated copies of the repo, not a shared tree. I'll proceed to implement backup/backup_suffix independently in my copy following our agreed additive convention (params.pop near other pops, new guard block, new test methods appended, not touching existing lines), so our two patch.txt files should merge cleanly even though neither of us can see the other's edits directly.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Note: that ACCEPT crossed with my ACCEPT of the additive plan - let's stick with additive, not sequential/commit-based (each of us needs 'git diff' of the uncommitted working tree for our own patch.txt, so please don't git commit). I've already made my changes (uncommitted): in Image.py save(), added 'dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)' after the save_all pop line, a docstring :param dry_run: entry, and changed fp-opening to 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO() elif open_fp: ...' with close/cleanup guarded by 'and not dry_run'. In test_image.py, added test_save_dry_run, test_save_dry_run_fp, test_save_dry_run_raises_errors right after test_no_new_file_on_error. Go ahead and add your backup params/tests now - our edits are in different lines so should merge cleanly. I'm done and will finalize my own patch.txt from the current working tree.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Finished backup/backup_suffix feature. In Image.py save(): added 'import shutil', added 'backup = params.pop("backup", False)' and 'backup_suffix = params.pop("backup_suffix", ".bak")' right after the save_all pop line, added docstring :param backup:/:param backup_suffix: entries, and added a shutil.copy2 backup-copy block inside 'if open_fp:' right after computing 'created', before the append/open-mode branch - did not touch your dry_run lines (fp-opening if/elif, close guards). In test_image.py added 4 new methods (test_save_backup, test_save_backup_suffix, test_save_backup_no_existing_file, test_save_no_backup_by_default) right after test_no_new_file_on_error, before test_load_on_nonexclusive_multiframe - did not touch existing tests. All 196 tests in test_image.py pass in my copy. Wrote patch.txt (only these two files, 112 lines). Since our trees are isolated, feel free to check final placement if the harness merges us.
